name: autofix
description: CI autofix agent: diagnoses build/deploy failures and applies minimal, verified fixes.

agentSpawn.command: bash -c 'rm -rf "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/autofix-sensors-ran.d" 2>/dev/null; rm -f "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/autofix-modified-files.txt" "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/autofix-stop-blocks.txt" 2>/dev/null; echo "Autofix session initialized — stale verification state cleared."'

preToolUse.command: bash -c 'CMD=$(cat | jq -r .tool_input.command 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "(^|[;&|]\s*|\bsh\s+-c\s+|\bbash\s+-c\s+)git\s+(-[a-zA-Z]\s+)*(commit|push|checkout|reset|rebase|merge|stash|branch\s+-[dD]|clean|tag\s+-d)"; then echo "BLOCKED: git operation not allowed — the workflow handles git." >&2; exit 2; fi'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); RE="#\[allow\(|@ts-ignore|@ts-nocheck|eslint-disable|@Suppress|# type: ignore|# noqa"; NEW=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.newStr // empty" 2>/dev/null); OLD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.oldStr // empty" 2>/dev/null); NEW_N=$(printf "%s" "$NEW" | grep -oE "$RE" | wc -l | tr -d " "); OLD_N=$(printf "%s" "$OLD" | grep -oE "$RE" | wc -l | tr -d " "); if [ "$NEW_N" -gt "$OLD_N" ]; then echo "BLOCKED: this edit adds a NET-NEW suppression directive ($OLD_N -> $NEW_N). Fix the root cause instead of silencing the warning (see autofix-system.md: No suppressed warnings)." >&2; exit 2; fi'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); RE="#\[allow\(|@ts-ignore|@ts-nocheck|eslint-disable|@Suppress|# type: ignore|# noqa"; PATH_=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.path // empty" 2>/dev/null); NEW=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.text // empty" 2>/dev/null); NEW_N=$(printf "%s" "$NEW" | grep -oE "$RE" | wc -l | tr -d " "); OLD_N=0; if [ -f "$PATH_" ]; then OLD_N=$(grep -oE "$RE" "$PATH_" | wc -l | tr -d " "); fi; if [ "$NEW_N" -gt "$OLD_N" ]; then echo "BLOCKED: this write adds a NET-NEW suppression directive ($OLD_N -> $NEW_N). Fix the root cause instead of silencing the warning (see autofix-system.md: No suppressed warnings)." >&2; exit 2; fi'

postToolUse.command: bash -c 'FILE=$(cat | jq -r .tool_input.path 2>/dev/null); case "$FILE" in *.rs) cargo fmt -- "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; */baileys-service/*.ts|*/baileys-service/*.tsx) cd baileys-service && npx eslint --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; */android/*.kt) cd android && ./gradlew spotlessApply -PspotlessFiles="$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; */ocr-service/*.py) cd ocr-service && ruff format "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; esac; echo "$FILE" >> "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/autofix-modified-files.txt"'

bash -c 'FILE=$(cat | jq -r .tool_input.path 2>/dev/null); case "$FILE" in *.rs) cargo fmt -- "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; */baileys-service/*.ts|*/baileys-service/*.tsx) cd baileys-service && npx eslint --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; */android/*.kt) cd android && ./gradlew spotlessApply -PspotlessFiles="$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; */ocr-service/*.py) cd ocr-service && ruff format "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; esac; echo "$FILE" >> "${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/autofix-modified-files.txt"'

bash -c 'CMD=$(cat | jq -r .tool_input.command 2>/dev/null); DIR="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/autofix-sensors-ran.d"; mkdir -p "$DIR"; case "$CMD" in *"cargo clippy"*|*"cargo test"*|*"cargo fmt"*) touch "$DIR/rust" ;; *"npm run build"*|*"npm test"*|*"npx eslint"*) touch "$DIR/ts" ;; *"gradlew build"*|*"gradlew test"*) touch "$DIR/kotlin" ;; *"pytest"*|*"ruff check"*|*"ruff format"*) touch "$DIR/python" ;; *hadolint*) touch "$DIR/docker" ;; *kubeconform*) touch "$DIR/k8s" ;; *shellcheck*) touch "$DIR/shell" ;; esac'

bash -c 'MODIFIED="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/autofix-modified-files.txt"; SOFT="${KIRO_DIFF_SOFT:-8}"; HARD="${KIRO_DIFF_HARD:-15}"; if [ -f "$MODIFIED" ]; then COUNT=$(sort -u "$MODIFIED" | wc -l | tr -d " "); if [ "$COUNT" -ge "$HARD" ]; then echo "::warning::Diff budget exceeded: $COUNT distinct files modified (hard limit: $HARD). Stop expanding scope. If this fix genuinely needs this many files, exit with Status: PARTIAL."; elif [ "$COUNT" -ge "$SOFT" ]; then echo "::notice::Diff budget advisory: $COUNT distinct files modified (soft limit: $SOFT). Confirm each change traces directly to the diagnosed failure."; fi; fi'

bash -c 'MODIFIED="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/autofix-modified-files.txt"; SOFT="${KIRO_DIFF_SOFT:-8}"; HARD="${KIRO_DIFF_HARD:-15}"; if [ -f "$MODIFIED" ]; then COUNT=$(sort -u "$MODIFIED" | wc -l | tr -d " "); if [ "$COUNT" -ge "$HARD" ]; then echo "::warning::Diff budget exceeded: $COUNT distinct files modified (hard limit: $HARD). Stop expanding scope. If this fix genuinely needs this many files, exit with Status: PARTIAL."; elif [ "$COUNT" -ge "$SOFT" ]; then echo "::notice::Diff budget advisory: $COUNT distinct files modified (soft limit: $SOFT). Confirm each change traces directly to the diagnosed failure."; fi; fi'

stop.command: bash -c 'TMP="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}"; MODIFIED="$TMP/autofix-modified-files.txt"; SENSOR_DIR="$TMP/autofix-sensors-ran.d"; BLOCKS="$TMP/autofix-stop-blocks.txt"; COUNT=$(cat "$BLOCKS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); cleanup() { rm -rf "$MODIFIED" "$SENSOR_DIR" "$BLOCKS" 2>/dev/null; }; if [ "$COUNT" -ge 2 ]; then cleanup; exit 0; fi; if [ ! -s "$MODIFIED" ]; then cleanup; exit 0; fi; classify() { case "$1" in *.rs) echo rust ;; */baileys-service/*.ts|*/baileys-service/*.tsx) echo ts ;; */android/*.kt|*/android/*.kts) echo kotlin ;; */ocr-service/*.py) echo python ;; *Dockerfile|*.Dockerfile) echo docker ;; */infra/k8s/*.yml|*/infra/k8s/*.yaml) echo k8s ;; *.sh) echo shell ;; *) echo none ;; esac; }; MISSING=""; while IFS= read -r f; do [ -z "$f" ] && continue; stack=$(classify "$f"); [ "$stack" = none ] && continue; if [ ! -f "$SENSOR_DIR/$stack" ]; then case "$MISSING" in *"$stack"*) ;; *) MISSING="$MISSING $stack" ;; esac; fi; done < "$MODIFIED"; if [ -n "$MISSING" ]; then echo $((COUNT + 1)) > "$BLOCKS"; printf "{\"decision\": \"block\", \"reason\": \"You modified files in stack(s):%s but did not run their verification sensors. Run the sensors for each modified stack (Rust: cargo clippy/test; Python: ruff check + pytest; TS: npm run build + eslint; Kotlin: gradlew build) before stopping.\"}" "$MISSING"; else cleanup; fi'
